title: Sr. Software Engineer-7980
description_html: FreeWheel, a Comcast company, provides comprehensive ad platforms for publishers, advertisers, and media buyers. Powered by premium video content, robust data, and advanced technology, we’re making it easier for buyers and sellers to transact across all screens, data types, and sales channels. As a global company, we have offices in nine countries and can insert advertisements around the world.        Job Summary      Job Description   COMPANY:                      Comcast Cable Communications, LLC  DUTIES:                            Provide technical leadership in designing and developing new software and web applications; develop backend software using Go; develop frontend software using React, JavaScript, HTML, CSS, and Typescript; perform Unit and Integration Testing using gomock, Cypress, and Playwright framework; develop software using MySQL, Redis, AWS, and Agile development methodologies; build CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and Docker; support applications under development and customize current applications; assist with the software update process for existing applications, and roll-outs of software releases; analyze, test, and assist with the integration of new applications; document all development activity; research, write, and edit documentation and technical requirements, including software designs, evaluation plans, test results, technical manuals, and formal recommendations and reports; monitor and evaluate competitive applications and products; review literature, patents, and current practices relevant to the solution of assigned projects; collaborate with project stakeholders to identify product and technical requirements; conduct analysis to determine integration needs; work with the Quality Assurance team to determine if applications fit specification and technical requirements; and guide and mentor junior-level engineers. Position is eligible to work remotely one or more days per week, per company policy.    REQUIREMENTS:            Bachelor ’ s degree, or foreign equivalent, in Computer Science, Engineering, or related technical field, and five (5) years of experience developing backend software using Go; of which three (3) years of experience include developing frontend software using React, Typescript, JavaScript, HTML, and CSS; developing software using MySQL, Redis, AWS, and Agile development methodologies; and building CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and Docker; of which two (2) years include performing Unit and Integration Testing using gomock and Playwright framework.   Disclaimer:  This information has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees in this role. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities and qualifications.       Skills  Go Programming Language, Redis, TypeScript     We believe that benefits should connect you to the support you need when it matters most, and should help you care for those who matter most. title: Senior Applied AI Engineer
location: PA - Philadelphia, 1701 John F Kennedy Blvd
description_html: Make your mark at Comcast -- a Fortune 30 global media and technology company. From the connectivity and platforms we provide, to the content and experiences we create, we reach hundreds of millions of customers, viewers, and guests worldwide. Become part of our award-winning technology team that turns big ideas into cutting-edge products, platforms, and solutions that our customers love. We create space to innovate, and we recognize, reward, and invest in your ideas, while ensuring you can proudly bring your authentic self to the workplace. Join us. You’ll do the best work of your career right here at Comcast. (In most cases, Comcast prefers to have employees on-site collaborating unless the team has been designated as virtual due to the nature of their work. If a position is listed with both office locations and virtual offerings, Comcast may be willing to consider candidates who live greater than 100 miles from the office for the remote option.)        Job Summary  We're seeking a Senior Applied AI Engineer to join our Cloud Security Engineering team and help shape the next generation of intelligent platforms that drive security outcomes across the organization. In this role, you'll design and build AI-powered solutions that transform large volumes of technical and business data into actionable insights, automated workflows, and measurable results.  While the work supports cybersecurity initiatives, this is fundamentally a software engineering and applied AI role focused on creating scalable platforms, intelligent automation, and modern cloud-native services that empower engineering teams to work smarter and move faster.   As a Senior Applied AI Engineer, you'll own critical technical initiatives from concept through production, driving innovation across AI, automation, and software engineering.    Job Description    What You'll Do     Design and build intelligent software platforms that leverage AI-assisted reasoning, automation, and workflow orchestration.    Develop self-service capabilities that help teams investigate issues, implement recommendations, and execute repeatable processes with minimal manual effort.    Integrate data and services across cloud platforms, internal systems, APIs, and SaaS applications.    Create scalable backend services and APIs that support complex business workflows and operational decision-making.    Lead architecture and implementation decisions across distributed systems, workflow automation, and data pipelines.    Build reliable systems with observability, resiliency, fault tolerance, and operational excellence in mind.    Partner with software engineers, platform teams, product partners, and security stakeholders to deliver impactful solutions.    Research, prototype, and apply emerging AI technologies to solve real-world business and engineering challenges.    Mentor engineers, influence technical direction, and help elevate engineering best practices across the organization.       What You'll Need:      7+ years of software engineering experience designing, building, and supporting production systems.    Strong Python development skills and experience building backend APIs and services using FastAPI or similar frameworks.    Experience with PostgreSQL, data modeling, ORM frameworks, and scalable data processing systems.    Proven ability to design distributed, service-oriented architectures, including workflow orchestration, asynchronous processing, and resilient application design.    Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to translate complex requirements into scalable technical solutions.       Cloud & Platform Engineering     Experience building and operating applications in AWS, including services such as EC2, ECS/Fargate, Lambda, RDS, S3, IAM, and CloudWatch.    Experience with Docker, containerized application delivery, and CI/CD pipelines.    Knowledge of automated testing, deployment strategies, infrastructure-as-code, and modern DevOps practices.    Experience implementing observability, reliability, and operational best practices for production systems.     Preferred Qualifications     Experience developing AI-powered applications, workflow automation platforms, or intelligent decision-support systems.    Familiarity with cybersecurity, risk management, compliance, or cloud security concepts.    Experience with React/TypeScript and modern web application development.    Experience with data and analytics platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, OpenSearch, or Elasticsearch.         Disclaimer        This information has been designed to   indicate   the general nature and level of work performed by employees in this role. It is not designed to   contain   or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and qualifications.         Skills  Agentic AI, Amazon Web Services (AWS), Cloud DevOps, Infrastructure As Code (IaC), Python Software Development, System Designs, Technical Leadership     We believe that benefits should connect you to the support you need when it matters most, and should help you care for those who matter most. That's why we provide an array of options, expert guidance and always-on tools that are personalized to meet the needs of your reality—to help support you physically, financially and emotionally through the big milestones and in your everyday life.   Please visit the  benefits summary  on our careers site for more details.     Education  Bachelor's Degree    While possessing the stated degree is preferred, Comcast also may consider applicants who hold some combination of coursework and experience, or who have extensive related professional experience.    Certifications  (if applicable)     Relevant Work Experience  7-10 Years    Comcast is an equal opportunity workplace.  We will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, genetic information, or any other basis protected by applicable law.

title: Lead Software Engineer-0773
description_html: FreeWheel, a Comcast company, provides comprehensive ad platforms for publishers, advertisers, and media buyers. Powered by premium video content, robust data, and advanced technology, we’re making it easier for buyers and sellers to transact across all screens, data types, and sales channels. As a global company, we have offices in nine countries and can insert advertisements around the world.        Job Summary      Job Description   DUTIES:                            Provide technical leadership in designing and developing new software and web applications; work on ad tech systems, covering ad-serving data indexing engines, ad selection algorithms, and audience targeting pipelines; optimize relational (MySQL, Aurora) and NoSQL (Aerospike, DynamoDB, ETCD, Neo4J, RocksDB) databases through thoughtful schema design, efficient query crafting, and cache fine-tuning; leverage profiling tools (sanitizer, perf and gprof), along with custom benchmarks and tracing, to diagnose and optimize CPU, memory, and I/O performance issues; architect and develop scalable, low-latency C++ applications on Linux, designed to handle high-throughput, high-QPS workloads; architect, implement, and optimize core services across distributed infrastructure, driving improvements in throughput, reliability, and overall system efficiency; use cloud-native tools such as Kubernetes, Docker, and Terraform, alongside key AWS services including ELB, EKS, S3, DynamoDB, and Kinesis; and work with SRE/DevOps teams to instrument systems (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK, Loki stack); support applications under development and customize current applications; assist with the software update process for existing applications, and roll-outs of software releases; analyze, test, and assist with the integration of new applications; document all development activity; research, write, and edit documentation and technical requirements, including software designs, evaluation plans, test results, technical manuals, and formal recommendations and reports; monitor and evaluate competitive applications and products; review literature, patents, and current practices relevant to the solution of assigned projects; collaborate with project stakeholders to identify product and technical requirements; conduct analysis to determine integration needs; work with the Quality Assurance team to determine if applications fit specification and technical requirements; and guide and mentor junior-level engineers. Position is eligible to work remotely one or more days per week, per company policy.    REQUIREMENTS:            Bachelor’s degree, or foreign equivalent, in Computer Science, Engineering, or related technical field, and seven (7) years of experience developing software for ad tech systems, including ad-serving data indexing engines, ad selection algorithms, and audience targeting pipelines; caching and optimizing relational databases including MySQL and Aurora, and NoSQL databases including Aerospike, DynamoDB, ETCD, Neo4J, and RocksDB; using sanitizer, perf, and gprof profiling tools for performance tuning; architecting and developing scalable, low-latency C++ applications on Linux; performing end to end system design and optimization; of which five (5) years include using cloud-native tools including Kubernetes, Docker, and Terraform, and AWS services including ELB, EKS, S3, DynamoDB, and Kinesis; and monitoring and troubleshooting systems using Prometheus, Grafana, ELK, and Loki stack.    Disclaimer:  This information has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees in this role. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities and qualifications.       Skills  Aerospike Database, Docker (Software), Grafana     We believe that benefits should connect you to the support you need when it matters most, and should help you care for those who matter most. title: Lead Software Engineer (Data)-4555
description_html: FreeWheel, a Comcast company, provides comprehensive ad platforms for publishers, advertisers, and media buyers. Powered by premium video content, robust data, and advanced technology, we’re making it easier for buyers and sellers to transact across all screens, data types, and sales channels. As a global company, we have offices in nine countries and can insert advertisements around the world.        Job Summary      Job Description   DUTIES:                            Provide technical leadership to a team responsible for developing data products using Golang and Python; writing SQL queries for data exploration in Clickhouse; querying data using Snowflake; deploying data products to AWS cloud platform including EKS, EMR, RDS, and S3; scheduling and monitoring workflows using Airflow; developing dashboards using Looker; performing version control using GitHub; building CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins; and performing application monitoring and alerting using Grafana; apply specialized knowledge of our Data platforms; provide leadership throughout the design process and assists in guidance with regards to practices, procedures and techniques; plan and design new software and web applications; analyze, test and assist with the integration of new applications; oversee the documentation of all development activity; train non-technical personnel; assist with tracking performance metrics; provide guidance and support to other engineers; integrate knowledge of business and functional priorities; act as a key contributor in a complex and crucial environment; collaborate with project stakeholders to identify product and technical requirements; conducts analysis to determine integration needs; design new software and web applications, supports applications under development and customizes current applications; develop software update processes for existing applications; assist in the roll-out of software releases; oversee the researching, writing and editing of documentation and technical requirements, including software designs, evaluation plans, test results, technical manuals and formal recommendations and reports; keep current with technological developments within the industry; monitor and evaluate competitive applications and products; review literature, patents and current practices relevant to the solution of assigned projects; assist in tracking and evaluating performance metrics; ensure team delivers software on time, to specification and within budget; work with Quality Assurance team to determine if applications fit specification and technical requirements; test and evaluate systems, subsystems and components; act as a technical contact and liaison for outside vendors and/or customers; present and defend architectural, design and technical choices to internal and external audiences; and train junior Software Development Engineers on internally developed software applications. Position is eligible to work remotely one or more days per week, per company policy.    REQUIREMENTS:            Bachelor’s degree, or foreign equivalent, in Computer Science, Engineering, or related technical field, and seven (7) years of experience developing data products using Golang and Python; of which five (5) years include writing SQL queries for data exploration in Clickhouse; querying data using Snowflake; deploying data products to AWS cloud platform including EKS, EMR, RDS, and S3; scheduling and monitoring workflows using Airflow; developing dashboards using Looker; performing version control using GitHub; of which two (2) years include building CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins; and performing application monitoring and alerting using Grafana. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities and qualifications.
